spreads.name: IT Exporters vs Private Banks
spreads.notes: INR weakening favours dollar earners over domestic rate-sensitives

spreads.name: Pharma Exporters vs Domestic Consumer
spreads.notes: INR weakness + global risk-off: export pharma defensive vs domestic consumption

spreads.name: FII Exit Canary
spreads.notes: FII sustained outflow: dollar earners + defensives vs FII-darling domestic cyclicals

spreads.name: Domestic Finance vs Export IT
spreads.notes: Weak dollar = EM inflows, banks rally; IT loses USD translation benefit

spreads.name: Real Assets vs Dollar Earners
spreads.notes: Weak dollar lifts commodity prices in USD; IT exporters lose FX tailwind

spreads.name: IT Exporters vs Auto
spreads.notes: Strong dollar = IT earns more in INR terms; auto hit by input cost + demand

spreads.name: Rate Beneficiaries vs Upstream
spreads.notes: Rate cuts boost lending margins, real estate demand; commodity plays unrelated

spreads.name: Upstream vs Rate Sensitives
spreads.notes: Rate hikes crush leveraged sectors; commodity/upstream defensive

spreads.name: Defensive Core vs High Beta
spreads.notes: Flight to safety: FMCG + pharma + commodity vs leveraged high-beta names

spreads.name: Domestic India vs Export IT
spreads.notes: US recession = IT budget cuts; domestic consumption resilient

spreads.name: FII Return Play
spreads.notes: FII return flow: buy underowned private banks/NBFC, sell defensives they never owned

spreads.name: Gold Proxy vs Cyclicals
spreads.notes: Domestic defensives as gold proxy; metals crash on global demand fear
